text: Twelve weeks of complete detraining reduces cycling VO2max by approximately 9.1% and running VO2max by approximately 10.9% in a highly trained master endurance athlete.
Running economy deteriorates substantially after 12 weeks of detraining, with a 22% increase in oxygen cost at a given pace.
Maximal aerobic velocity declines by approximately 8.6% after 12 weeks of detraining.
Twelve weeks of detraining increases body fat percentage from 10.5% to 13.8% and reduces lean mass by 2.2 kg in a master endurance athlete.
specific_value: 10.5% to 13.8% body fat, -2.2 kg lean mass

text: Knee extensor maximal strength decreases by an average of 8.2% after 12 weeks of detraining.
Twelve weeks of progressive structured retraining is sufficient to restore VO2max to baseline after 12 weeks of complete detraining in a lifelong master endurance athlete.
Running economy and lean mass are slower to recover than VO2max after a detraining period, remaining below baseline even after 12 weeks of retraining.
